request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will the SEC approve a spot Solana ETF in 2025?
Kind: retrospective
Timeline (20 events):
- 2025-12-29 [background] Final Rule Delegation of Authority to the Director of the Division of Investment Management IA-6934 — Delegation to the DIM director could speed/streamline ETF approval processes, modestly increasing the chance a spot SOL ETF is approved before 2025-12-31.
- 2025-12-12 [background] Litigation Releases: Nathan Gauvin; Blackridge, LLC; Gray Digital Capital Management USA, LLC; Gray Digital Technologies, LLC — An SEC enforcement action against crypto firms signals heightened scrutiny of crypto industry, slightly lowering probability of timely spot SOL ETF approvals.
- 2025-12-05 [background] SEC Announces Agenda and Panelists for Roundtable on Financial Surveillance and Privacy — SEC roundtable on surveillance/privacy (with pro-crypto Commissioner Peirce) signals agency engagement with crypto issues, slightly nudging odds but is not determinative of an ETF approval.
- 2025-12-04 [background] Remarks at the Meeting of the Investor Advisory Committee — General SEC Investor Advisory Committee remarks may signal commissioner views on spot-ETF policy but do not constitute an approval order.
- 2025-11-12 [background] Remarks at the 2025 U.S. Treasury Market Conference — Remarks on Treasury markets by SEC officials contain no mention of spot Solana ETFs or exchange approval actions, so minimal impact.
- 2025-10-03 [background] Platinum Analytics Cayman Limited — Mentions the SEC and Nasdaq but contains no explicit Solana/spot-ETF info; only marginally shifts odds as general regulatory/Nasdaq activity.
- 2025-09-30 [background] Poking Holes: Statement in Response to No-Action Relief for State Trust Companies Acting as Crypto Asset Custodians — SEC statement on state-trust crypto custodians affects custody clarity for spot SOL ETFs, modestly altering the likelihood of SEC approval.
- 2025-09-30 [background] Cultivating Confidence: The Role of Custody in Institutional Confidence – Public Trust and Oversight; Remarks before the Digital Assets Summit Singapore — SEC remarks on custody and institutional confidence signal the regulator is focusing on crypto custody solutions—modestly raising probability of spot SOL ETF approval.
- 2025-09-19 [background] _Federal Register_ version — Federal Register posting of an SEC release on 2025-09-19 could be the effective approval notice for a spot Solana ETF, but metadata lacks ETF/exchange specifics.
- 2025-09-17 [background] SEC Approves Generic Listing Standards for Commodity-Based Trust Shares — SEC's approval of generic commodity-trust listing standards lowers regulatory hurdles for exchanges to list spot commodity ETFs, increasing odds for a SOL ETF.
- 2025-09-05 [background] Joint Statement from the Chairman of the SEC and Acting Chairman of the CFTC — A joint SEC‑CFTC statement on crypto jurisdiction could shift SEC's stance on token treatment and modestly change odds of a spot SOL ETF approval, but it doesn't approve one.
- 2025-09-05 [background] CFTC and SEC Issue Joint Statement on Regulatory Harmonization Efforts — Joint SEC–CFTC harmonization statement suggests improved inter-agency crypto coordination, slightly raising chance of an SEC spot-SOL ETF approval but not decisive.
- 2025-07-30 [background] Acting Chairman Pham Lauds Presidential Working Group Recommendations to Usher in Golden Age of Crypto in the U.S. — CFTC praise of PWG recommendations signals pro-crypto regulatory momentum and modestly raises the probability the SEC would approve a spot Solana ETF, but it's not direct.
- 2025-06-13 [background] Brian Daly Named Director of Division of Investment Management — New director of the SEC’s Division of Investment Management could influence ETF registration policy and guidance relevant to spot-SOL ETF approvals; indirect, small effect.
- 2025-06-10 [background] SEC to Resume Processing of Registration Applications From Swiss-Based Investment Advisers — Resuming SEC processing of Swiss adviser registrations slightly raises odds if Swiss sponsors (e.g., 21Shares) need U.S. registration to support a U.S. spot SOL ETF, but it's indirect.
- 2025-06-03 [background] Testimony Before the United States Senate Appropriations Subcommittee on Financial Services and General Government — SEC testimony may reveal commissioners' views or policy direction on crypto/ETF approvals, modestly affecting odds but not directly approving SOL ETF.
- 2025-04-16 [background] Form N-PORT and Form N-CEN Reporting; Guidance on Open-End Fund Liquidity Risk Investment Management — SEC guidance tightening liquidity reporting for open-end funds modestly raises scrutiny on funds holding crypto, slightly lowering chances of spot SOL ETF approvals.
- 2025-02-04 [background] SEC Crypto Task Force Designation (PDF) — SEC designating a crypto task force signals increased agency focus on crypto, modestly raising odds a spot SOL ETF could be reviewed/approved in 2025.
- 2024-09-24 [background] Testimony of the Securities and Exchange Commission Before the United States House of Representatives Committee on Financial Services — SEC commissioners' testimony can signal the agency's stance on crypto ETFs and modestly shift the chance of a spot Solana ETF approval, but it is not an approval.
- 2024-08-22 [background] 2024 — Multi-agency comments include the SEC/CFTC and inform crypto regulatory climate, but contain no specific SEC approval or exchange rule-change for a spot SOL ETF.

response.text: Through 2025, the SEC signaled engagement and groundwork: approving generic commodity-trust listing standards (9/17), issuing custody-focused remarks (9/30), and interagency coordination, while delegation to DIM (12/29) aimed to streamline reviews. But no order approving a spot Solana ETF was issued (the 9/19 Federal Register item lacked ETF specifics); enforcement actions and tighter fund-reporting scrutiny persisted, and the contract resolved No for 2025.
